topic: boat trip — first time taking it out on the Delaware

turns.content: took the boat out for the first time today. put it in up near Neshaminy and ran it down toward the city. perfect day for it.
That sounds like a great first outing. How did everything go with the boat — engine run smooth, no surprises?
engine ran clean. had a little trouble at the ramp getting her off the trailer but a guy next to me gave me a hand. once I was in the water it was good to go.
Loading and unloading at the ramp is always the trickiest part, especially the first few times. It gets easier with practice. How was the river itself — much traffic out there?
not bad. couple of sailboats. few kayakers. a barge came through and I gave it plenty of room. I'm not stupid. that thing is not stopping for me.

Smart move — those barges have a massive stopping distance and limited maneuverability. Giving them wide clearance is the right call every time. Did you do any fishing while you were out, or was this more of a shakedown run?

shakedown run. wanted to see how she handled. make sure everything worked before I commit to a fishing trip. check the electronics. test the radio. same as checking your equipment before a shift. you don't go in blind.
That's a solid approach — better to find any issues on a calm test run than when you're out fishing and the weather turns. How did the electronics look? GPS, fish finder, everything working?

GPS worked fine. fish finder lit up. VHF is good. only thing is the depth gauge was reading funny near the marina so I need to check the transducer. probably just needs to be cleaned. overall I'm happy with it.

A transducer reading off near the marina could just be shallow water interference, air bubbles, or some fouling on the sensor. A quick cleaning should sort it out. If it reads fine in deeper water, that confirms it's just a near-shore issue. Sounds like the boat is in good shape overall.

yeah I think it was just the shallow water near the ramp. once I got out in the channel it settled down. listen I haven't felt that relaxed in a while. just me and the water and nothing else. no calls. no radio. no problems. I could get used to that.
There's nothing quite like being out on the water for clearing your head. Are you planning to do any fishing next time out, or still getting comfortable with the boat first?
next trip is fishing. I want to try the back channels near Marcus Hook. guy at the bait shop said the stripers are running.
Marcus Hook is a solid spot — the channels around there get good striper action in the spring. Live bait or lures?
I'll probably run bucktails and keep it simple. I haven't fished in a while so I'm not going to pretend I know what I'm doing with live bait rigs yet. keep it basic. see what happens.

Bucktails are a great choice for stripers — simple, effective, and hard to mess up. White or chartreuse in that water. Bounce them off the bottom near structure and you'll connect. Enjoy the next trip — sounds like you've earned some time on the water.
